name: jj

metadata.name: jj
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: Apache Software License

documentation:
# jj

[![Codecov](https://img.shields.io/codecov/c/github/jj-mock/jj/master.svg?style=flat-square)](https://codecov.io/gh/jj-mock/jj)
[![PyPI](https://img.shields.io/pypi/v/jj.svg?style=flat-square)](https://pypi.python.org/pypi/jj)
[![PyPI - Downloads](https://img.shields.io/pypi/dm/jj?style=flat-square)](https://pypi.python.org/pypi/jj)
[![Python Version](https://img.shields.io/pypi/pyversions/jj.svg?style=flat-square)](https://pypi.python.org/pypi/jj)

JJ is a remote HTTP mock library that simulates HTTP responses for incoming requests. It enables users to define conditions for incoming HTTP requests and specify the responses that should be returned. The library operates on a client-server architecture, allowing for the server to be run locally or deployed remotely, catering to diverse testing environments.

## Features
- **Flexible Request Matching**: Define complex conditions to match incoming HTTP requests.
- **Customizable Responses**: Tailor responses to suit your testing needs.
- **Client-Server Architecture**: Run your mock server locally or deploy it remotely.
- **Real-time Interaction**: Modify mocking behavior on-the-fly without server restarts.

## Documentation
For comprehensive guidance on utilizing JJ, including installation, setup, and usage instructions, visit the [documentation page](https://jj-mock.io/docs/quick-start).

functions.name: jj.handlers._handler.default_handler
documentation:
Handle unmatched requests with a default 404 response.

This asynchronous function is used as the default handler for HTTP requests
that do not match any defined routes or handlers. It returns a `Response` object
with a 404 status code, indicating that the requested resource was not found.

```
param request

The incoming HTTP request that triggered the default handler.

return

A  object with a 404 status code.
```

classes.name: jj.expiration_policy._expiration_policy.ExpirationPolicy

methods.name: jj.expiration_policy._expiration_policy.ExpirationPolicy.is_expired
documentation:
Determine whether the response associated with this policy has expired.

```
param request

The request to evaluate against the expiration criteria.

return

 if the policy deems the response expired, otherwise .

raises NotImplementedError

If this method is not implemented by a subclass.
```

methods.name: jj.expiration_policy._expiration_policy.ExpirationPolicy.__repr__
documentation:
Return a string representation of the ExpirationPolicy instance.

```
return

A string representation of the instance.
```

Base class for defining expiration policies in HTTP mocking.

An expiration policy determines if a mocked response should still be
considered valid based on the criteria defined by its implementation.

classes.name: jj.expiration_policy._expire_never.ExpireNever

methods.name: jj.expiration_policy._expire_never.ExpireNever.is_expired
documentation:
Check if the mock has expired. Always returns `False` as this policy never expires.

```
param request

The current request being evaluated (not used in this policy).

return

, indicating the mock never expires.
```

methods.name: jj.expiration_policy._expire_never.ExpireNever.__repr__
documentation:
Return a string representation of the ExpireNever instance.

```
return

A string representation of the instance.
```

methods.name: jj.expiration_policy._expire_never.ExpireNever.__packed__
documentation:
Serialize the ExpireNever instance for packing.

```
return

A dictionary containing the serialized form of the instance.
```

methods.name: jj.expiration_policy._expire_never.ExpireNever.__unpacked__
documentation:
Unpack a serialized ExpireNever instance.

```
return

A new instance of ExpireNever.
```

Represents a policy where a mocked response never expires.

This policy ensures that the mocked response is always valid, regardless
of how many requests have been made.

classes.name: jj.matchers.logical_matchers._logical_matcher.LogicalMatcher
documentation:
Serves as a base class for logical matchers that combine multiple matchers.

Logical matchers, such as `AnyMatcher` and `AllMatcher`, use this class as
a foundation to combine several `ResolvableMatcher` instances, enabling
the creation of more complex matching rules.

classes.name: jj.expiration_policy._expire_after_requests.ExpireAfterRequests

methods.name: jj.expiration_policy._expire_after_requests.ExpireAfterRequests.__init__
documentation:
Initialize the ExpireAfterRequests policy with a maximum number of allowed requests.

```
param max_requests_count

The maximum number of requests allowed before the mock expires.
Must be greater than 0.

raises AssertionError

If  is less than or equal to 0.
```

methods.name: jj.expiration_policy._expire_after_requests.ExpireAfterRequests.max_requests_count
documentation:
Return the maximum number of requests allowed before expiration.

```
return

The maximum number of requests.
```

methods.name: jj.expiration_policy._expire_after_requests.ExpireAfterRequests.is_expired
documentation:
Check if the mock has expired based on the number of requests made.

```
param request

The current request being checked against the expiration policy.

return

 if the mock has expired, otherwise .
```

methods.name: jj.expiration_policy._expire_after_requests.ExpireAfterRequests.__repr__
documentation:
Return a string representation of the ExpireAfterRequests instance.

```
return

A string representation of the instance.
```

methods.name: jj.expiration_policy._expire_after_requests.ExpireAfterRequests.__packed__
documentation:
Serialize the ExpireAfterRequests instance for packing.

```
return

A dictionary containing the serialized form of the instance.
```

methods.name: jj.expiration_policy._expire_after_requests.ExpireAfterRequests.__unpacked__
documentation:
Unpack a serialized ExpireAfterRequests instance.

```
param max_requests_count

The maximum number of requests allowed before expiration.

param kwargs

Additional keyword arguments (ignored).

return

A new instance of ExpireAfterRequests.
```

Represents an expiration policy based on the number of requests.

This policy expires a mocked response after a specified number of requests.
Once the maximum request count is reached, the response is considered expired
and will no longer match subsequent requests.

classes.name: jj.matchers._resolvable_matcher.ResolvableMatcher

methods.name: jj.matchers._resolvable_matcher.ResolvableMatcher.__init__
documentation:
Initialize a ResolvableMatcher with a given resolver.

```
param resolver

The resolver responsible for handling the registration
of the matcher and its corresponding handler.
```

methods.name: jj.matchers._resolvable_matcher.ResolvableMatcher.match
documentation:
Determine if the incoming HTTP request matches the conditions of this matcher.

```
param request

The HTTP request to evaluate against the matcher.

return

 if the request matches, otherwise .

raises NotImplementedError

This method must be implemented in a subclass.
```

methods.name: jj.matchers._resolvable_matcher.ResolvableMatcher.__call__
documentation:
Register a handler function to be executed when a request matches.

```
param handler

The function that will handle requests matching this matcher.

return

The handler function, which is registered to the resolver.
```

methods.name: jj.matchers._resolvable_matcher.ResolvableMatcher.__repr__
documentation:
Return a string representation of the ResolvableMatcher instance.

```
return

A string describing the class and its resolver.
```

Provides a base class for matchers that can resolve HTTP requests.

This class defines the interface for a matcher that checks if an HTTP
request meets certain conditions. It registers the matcher to a resolver
and can be used to bind a handler function to matching requests.

documentation:
Initialize an AllMatcher with a list of matchers and a resolver.

```
param matchers

A list of matchers to evaluate. Matching succeeds only if
every matcher in this list returns .

param resolver

The resolver responsible for registering this matcher.

raises AssertionError

If the matchers list is empty.
```

```
return

```

```

 only if every matcher in the list returns , otherwise .
```

documentation:

```

```

methods.name: jj.matchers.logical_matchers._all_matcher.AllMatcher.__unpacked__
documentation:
Unpack an AllMatcher instance from its serialized form.

```
param matchers

The list of matchers to use for this instance.

param resolver

The resolver to bind this matcher to.

param kwargs

Additional keyword arguments (ignored).

return

A new instance of AllMatcher.
```

Matches an HTTP request if all provided matchers succeed.

This matcher combines multiple `ResolvableMatcher` instances, and it
returns `True` only if every matcher in the list successfully matches
the incoming HTTP request.

Matches HTTP requests based on the HTTP method.

This matcher checks if the incoming request uses a specified HTTP method
(e.g., GET, POST). It supports matching against a static method or an
attribute-based matcher.

```
param matchers
```

```
The resolver to bind this matcher to.

param kwargs

A new instance of AnyMatcher.
```

Matches an HTTP request if any one of the provided matchers succeeds.

This matcher combines multiple `ResolvableMatcher` instances, and it
returns `True` as soon as one of them successfully matches the incoming
HTTP request.

classes.name: jj.matchers.request_matchers._request_matcher.RequestMatcher
documentation:
Serves as a base class for specific HTTP request matchers.

Inherits from `ResolvableMatcher` and is intended to be subclassed by
matchers that deal with different aspects of an HTTP request, such as
method, path, headers, etc.

methods.name: jj.matchers.attribute_matchers._route_matcher._Resource.match
documentation:
Match the given path against the route pattern.

```
param path

The URL path to match.

return

A dictionary of matched segments if the path matches, otherwise .
```

A resource that defines the logic for matching URL paths.

This class extends `DynamicResource` from aiohttp and provides the
ability to match a URL path against a predefined route pattern.

```
"httponly", and "version".
```

classes.name: jj.responses._stream_response.StreamResponse

methods.name: jj.responses._stream_response.StreamResponse.__init__
documentation:
Initialize the StreamResponse object with the specified status, reason, and headers.

```
param status

The HTTP status code for the response (default is 200).

param reason

The reason phrase for the status code (optional).

param headers

A dictionary of headers for the response (optional). If no 
header is provided, it defaults to the current server version.
```

Represents an HTTP response that supports streaming.

The `StreamResponse` class extends `aiohttp.web.StreamResponse` and adds a default
`Server` header with the server version.

```
param status

The HTTP status code for the response (default is 200).

param reason

The reason phrase for the status code (optional).

param headers

Optional headers for the response, passed as a dictionary.
If no  header is provided, it defaults to the server version.
```

```
The HTTP method to match (e.g., "GET", "POST").

return
```

```
return
```

```
The path or a matcher for the path to match (e.g., "/users").

return
```

functions.name: jj._core.match_headers

functions.name: jj._core.match_params

functions.name: jj._core.match_all
documentation:
Match an HTTP request if all provided matchers succeed.

functions.name: jj._core.match_any

functions.name: jj._core.start

documentation:
Wait for server termination, handling the provided exceptions.

```

A sequence of exception types that should be handled to stop the server.
```

functions.name: jj._core.serve
documentation:
Serve the provided app and wait for its termination.

(e.g., by a keyboard interrupt).

```
param app

The application instance to run (default: ).

param host

The host address to bind to (optional).

param port

The port to bind to (optional).

param logger

The logger instance to use for logging (default: ).
```

classes.name: jj._core.App
documentation:
Represents the main application class, extending `BaseApp`.

This class uses the global `resolver` for request handling and matcher resolution.

classes.name: jj._core.Middleware
documentation:
Represents middleware used in the application, extending `BaseMiddleware`.

This middleware also uses the global `resolver` for matcher resolution and handling.

documentation:
Initialize the Response object.

```

The JSON object to be used as the response body.
If provided, overrides  and .

The raw body content of the response as a string or bytes.

param text

The plain text content of the response.

param status

The HTTP status code for the response (default is 200).

The reason phrase for the response status code.

A dictionary or list of headers to include in the response.
```

```
The content encoding if compression is enabled, otherwise None.
```

```
The Morsel object representing the cookie.

return

A dictionary representing the cookie's attributes.
```

methods.name: jj.responses._response.Response.copy
documentation:
Create a copy of the Response object.

This method clones the current response including its status, reason, headers,
cookies, and compression settings.

```
return

A new instance of the Response object.

raises AssertionError

If the response is already prepared.
```

methods.name: jj.responses._response.Response.get_body
documentation:
Get the body of the response.

This method provides backward compatibility for retrieving the response body
as bytes.

```
return

The response body as bytes.
```

methods.name: jj.responses._response.Response._prepare_hook
documentation:
Hook method to be executed before preparing the response.

This method allows customization before the response is sent to the client.

```
param request

The request object associated with the response.

return

The Response object itself.
```

methods.name: jj.responses._response.Response.prepare
documentation:
Prepare the response for sending to the client.

This method finalizes the response, making it ready to be transmitted.

```
param request

The incoming HTTP request.

return

The stream writer used to send the response.
```

methods.name: jj.responses._response.Response.__packed__
documentation:
Pack the Response object into a dictionary for serialization.

This method is used for converting the Response object into a serializable
format that can be transmitted or stored remotely.

```
return

A dictionary containing the packed response data.

raises AssertionError

If the response is already prepared.
```

methods.name: jj.responses._response.Response.__unpacked__
documentation:
Reconstruct a Response instance from unpacked parameters.

```
param status

The HTTP status code.

param reason

The reason phrase for the HTTP status.

param headers

A list of headers for the response.

param cookies

A list of cookie dictionaries.

param body

The response body as bytes.

param chunked

A flag indicating whether chunked encoding is enabled.

param compression

The content compression coding.

param kwargs

Additional keyword arguments (ignored).

return

A new Response object.
```

Represents an HTTP response that can handle various content types, including
JSON, plain text, and file streams, while supporting chunked encoding,
compression, and cookie management.

This class extends aiohttp's `web.Response` and adds additional functionality
for handling response bodies, cookies, headers, and content encoding. It also
supports serialization and deserialization for remote handling of responses.

variables.name: jj.responses._template_response.jinja2
documentation: type: ignore

classes.name: jj.responses._template_response.TemplateResponse

methods.name: jj.responses._template_response.TemplateResponse.__init__
documentation:
Initialize the TemplateResponse with optional body, headers, and status.

```
param body

The template string for the response body. Defaults to an empty string.

param headers

A dictionary of headers, which can also contain templates.

param status

The HTTP status code, which can be templated as well (default is 200).
```

methods.name: jj.responses._template_response.TemplateResponse._prepare_hook
documentation:
Prepare the response by rendering the templates for body, headers, and status.

This method is called before sending the response to the client. It renders
the templated body, headers, and status code using the request context.

```
param request

The incoming HTTP request object used for rendering templates.

return

The  object after template rendering is complete.

raises ImportError

If Jinja2 is not installed.
```

methods.name: jj.responses._template_response.TemplateResponse._render_value
documentation:
Render a single template value using the request context.

This method renders a given string value as a Jinja2 template, passing the
request object into the template context.

```
param value

The string value to be rendered as a template.

param request

The incoming request used for rendering the template.

return

The rendered template as a string.
```

methods.name: jj.responses._template_response.TemplateResponse.copy
documentation:
Create a copy of the TemplateResponse object.

```
return

A new instance of  with the same template configuration.

raises AssertionError

If the response is already prepared.
```

methods.name: jj.responses._template_response.TemplateResponse.__packed__
documentation:
Pack the TemplateResponse into a dictionary for serialization.

This method prepares the response for serialization by packing its templated
body, headers, and status into a dictionary.

```
return

A dictionary representing the packed TemplateResponse.
```

methods.name: jj.responses._template_response.TemplateResponse.__unpacked__
documentation:
Reconstruct a TemplateResponse instance from unpacked parameters.

```
param body

The template string for the response body.

param headers

A list of headers, each represented as a tuple of (key, value).

param status

The HTTP status code as a string or integer.

param kwargs

Additional keyword arguments (ignored).

return

A new instance of .
```

Represents an HTTP response where the body and headers can be templated.

The `TemplateResponse` class extends the `Response` class and allows for the use
of Jinja2 templates to dynamically render the response body and headers based on
the request context. It relies on Jinja2 as an optional dependency.

classes.name: jj.responses._relay_response.RelayResponse

methods.name: jj.responses._relay_response.RelayResponse.__init__
documentation:
Initialize the RelayResponse with a target URL.

```
param target

The base URL of the target server to which the request will be relayed.
```

methods.name: jj.responses._relay_response.RelayResponse.target
documentation:
Get the target URL for the relayed request.

```
return

The base URL of the target server.
```

methods.name: jj.responses._relay_response.RelayResponse._filter_headers
documentation:
Filter headers to exclude hop-by-hop and other headers not forwarded.

This method removes headers that should not be forwarded to the target server,
such as hop-by-hop headers (per RFC 2616, Section 13.5.1) and certain others
like `host` and `content-length`.

```
param headers

The original headers of the incoming request.

return

A new  containing only the headers that should be forwarded.
```

methods.name: jj.responses._relay_response.RelayResponse._do_target_request
documentation:
Perform the request to the target server and retrieve the response.

This method constructs a new request based on the incoming request, forwards it to
the target server, and retrieves the response. It also handles reading the response
body and returning necessary response details.

```
param request

The incoming client request to be relayed.

return

A tuple containing the status code, reason, filtered headers,
and the body of the target response.
```

methods.name: jj.responses._relay_response.RelayResponse._prepare_hook
documentation:
Prepare the relay response by performing the request to the target server.

This method is called during the response preparation phase and ensures that
the request is sent to the target server. It also processes the response from
the target, including setting the appropriate status, headers, and body.

```
param request

The incoming request to be relayed.

return

The  object after the target request is processed.
```

methods.name: jj.responses._relay_response.RelayResponse.copy
documentation:
Create a copy of the RelayResponse object.

This method clones the current `RelayResponse` object, ensuring that the target URL
is preserved.

```
return

A new instance of  with the same target URL.

raises AssertionError

If the response is already prepared.
```

methods.name: jj.responses._relay_response.RelayResponse.__packed__
documentation:
Pack the RelayResponse object for serialization.

This method packs the target URL into a dictionary, which can then be used for
serialization or transmission.

```
return

A dictionary containing the target URL.
```

methods.name: jj.responses._relay_response.RelayResponse.__unpacked__
documentation:
Reconstruct a RelayResponse instance from unpacked parameters.

```
param target

The target URL to relay requests to.

param kwargs

Additional keyword arguments (ignored).

return

A new  instance with the specified target.
```

Relays the request to a target server and returns the response.

The `RelayResponse` class is a special kind of response that forwards incoming
requests to a target server, retrieves the response from the target, and relays
it back to the client.

variables.name: jj.logs.logger
documentation: type: ignore

functions.name: jj._load_module._path_to_module_name
documentation:
Convert a file path to a valid Python module name.

This helper function transforms the given file path into a module name by removing
the file extension and converting the path structure into a dot-separated string,
as required for module names. If the path is absolute, the leading part of the
path is removed.

```
param path

The file path to be converted.

return

A string representing the module name derived from the path.
```

functions.name: jj._load_module.load_module
documentation:
Load a Python module from the specified file path.

This function dynamically loads a module from the given path, using the
importlib utilities to create and load the module. It converts the file path
into a valid module name and registers the module in `sys.modules`.

```
param path

The file path from which to load the module.

return

The loaded module as a  object.

raises ModuleNotFoundError

If the module cannot be found or loaded.
```

functions.name: jj._entry_point.run
documentation:
Run the remote HTTP mock server.

This function sets up an argument parser to configure the server's host, port,
and optionally load custom matcher modules. It retrieves values from the
environment or provided arguments and starts the `jj` mock server.

cookies, and compression settings, while ensuring the delay is preserved.

```

A new instance of the DelayedResponse object.

raises AssertionError

If the response is already prepared.
```

methods.name: jj.responses._delayed_response.DelayedResponse.get_body
documentation:
Get the body of the response.

This method provides backward compatibility for retrieving the response body as bytes.

```
return

The response body as bytes.
```

methods.name: jj.responses._delayed_response.DelayedResponse._prepare_hook
documentation:
Hook method to be executed before preparing the response.

This method checks for a configured delay and if present, waits for the specified
time before allowing the response to be prepared.

```
param request

The request object associated with the response.

return

The DelayedResponse object itself.
```

methods.name: jj.responses._delayed_response.DelayedResponse.prepare
documentation:
Prepare the response for sending to the client.

This method finalizes the response, taking into account any delay specified before
sending the response to the client.

```
param request

The incoming HTTP request.

return

The stream writer used to send the response.
```

methods.name: jj.responses._delayed_response.DelayedResponse.__packed__
documentation:
Pack the DelayedResponse object into a dictionary for serialization.

This method is used for converting the DelayedResponse object into a serializable
format that can be transmitted or stored remotely.

```
return

A dictionary containing the packed response data.

raises AssertionError

If the response is already prepared.
```

methods.name: jj.responses._delayed_response.DelayedResponse.__unpacked__
documentation:
Reconstruct a DelayedResponse instance from unpacked parameters.

```
param status

The HTTP status code.

param reason

The reason phrase for the HTTP status.

param headers

A list of headers for the response.

param cookies

A list of cookie dictionaries.

param body

The response body as bytes.

param chunked

A flag indicating whether chunked encoding is enabled.

param compression

The content compression coding.

param delay

The delay in seconds before the response is sent.

param kwargs

Additional keyword arguments (ignored).

return

A new DelayedResponse object.
```

Represents an HTTP response that can be delayed before sending.

The `DelayedResponse` class is an extension of aiohttp's `web.Response`,
allowing a delay to be added before the response is sent back to the client.
This is useful for simulating latency or testing timeouts in a client-server architecture.

modules.name: jj.http.codes
documentation: 1xx

classes.name: jj.requests._request.Request

methods.name: jj.requests._request.Request.__init__
documentation:
Initialize the Request object.

```
param args

Positional arguments to pass to the base .

param kwargs

Keyword arguments to pass to the base .
```

methods.name: jj.requests._request.Request.params
documentation:
Get the query parameters of the request.

This is a shorthand property that returns the same result as `self.query`.

```
return

A  object containing the query parameters.
```

methods.name: jj.requests._request.Request.segments
documentation:
Get the URL path segments.

This property holds dynamic URL segments that can be set and retrieved for the request.
If no segments are set, an empty dictionary is returned.

```
return

A dictionary containing the path segments of the request,
or an empty dictionary if not set.
```

methods.name: jj.requests._request.Request.segments
documentation:
Set the URL path segments.

This allows setting a dictionary of path segments for the request. Path segments
are often used when dealing with dynamic URL paths that contain variable placeholders.

```
param segments

A dictionary of path segments, or  to reset the segments.
```

methods.name: jj.requests._request.Request._prepare_hook
documentation:
Prepare the request before it is processed by the server.

This hook method can be used to perform any custom preparation or pre-processing
on the request object before it is sent for handling.

```
param response

The response object associated with the request.
```

Extends aiohttp's `web.Request` to add support for URL path segments and custom parameters.

This class enhances the base `web.Request` by adding a `segments` attribute for handling
dynamic URL path segments and provides a shorthand property for accessing query parameters.

functions.name: jj.mock._stacked.stacked
documentation:
Stack multiple mocks into a single context manager for easier management.

This context manager allows you to stack multiple mocks (either in a list or a dictionary)
into a single context, ensuring they are all activated upon entering and deactivated
upon exiting. It simplifies the process of managing multiple mocks simultaneously.

```
param mocks

A list or dictionary of  objects to be activated in the context.
If a list is provided, the mocks are returned as a list; if a dictionary is
provided, they are returned as a dictionary with the same keys.

return

Yields the activated mocks. If the input was a list, it yields a list of active mocks;
if the input was a dictionary, it yields a dictionary of active mocks
keyed by the same names.

raises TypeError

If the  parameter is not a list or a dictionary.
```
